calendar.matters:
Petition Under Probate Code §§17200, 15409, 15640 And 15660 For Order: 1. Naming Suc Trustee & 2. Est. Procedure For The Apptment Of Future Suc Trus
Off Calendar Aug-02-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sarah King for (petitioner) Thomas Werdel, Jr. Paul Jones (proposed successor trustee) also present.

The Court stated that this is a petition to appoint a successor trustee and for a procedure for the appointment of future successor trustees, that all of the beneficiaries have signed on to this and asked about the size of the trust. Ms. King stated that the only income is from a feeder trust, that there are no current assets, that it will fund when the four beneficiaries are deceased and that they are not sure how large the feeder trust is. The Court stated that everyone is on board with Mr. Jones being appointed trustee, that future successor trustees be appointed without bond and that it is not sure that this is in the best interest of the trust. Ms. King stated that the trust will terminate after the death of Mr. Werdel who is now 80 years old, that Mr. Werdel will continue as trustee for as long as he can and that Mr. Jones may never need to serve as trustee. The Court stated that the issue is if for some reason Mr. Jones has to appoint a successor trustee. Ms. King stated that the remainder beneficiaries would choose successor trustees and that they would choose wisely. The Court stated that it just does not know who it might be and if that person should be allowed to serve without bond and that the succession language should not apply to successors after Mr. Jones. Ms. King stated that they have done this in another trust that this Court granted. The Court stated that it will grant the petition for appointment of successor trustee, etc. except that the language is to be modified to say that the successor to Mr. Jones would not necessarily serve without bond. Ms. King is to submit a proposed order.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)
